jokes.text: I swear, that tie is longer than a steamrolled green snake!
jokes.setup: Hermes is helping tie Dwight's tie
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific simile about the tie's length
jokes.explanation: Creative, vivid simile that's both specific and absurd. Good economy of language.

jokes.text: It's Dwight's first day at the Young Bureaucrats Club. He's a real check off the old box. Yum again.
jokes.setup: Hermes is proud of his son joining bureaucracy
jokes.punchline: Bureaucracy pun ('check off the old box') followed by satisfied 'Yum'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only Hermes would find bureaucracy this satisfying. The 'Yum' sells his genuine pleasure.

jokes.text: You have no idea how proud I am that you want to follow in my compulsively even footsteps, son. Eh, your fax made it pretty clear.
jokes.setup: Hermes expressing pride in overly bureaucratic language
jokes.punchline: Dwight's flat response that the fax already communicated this
jokes.explanation: Great character work - Hermes' bureaucratic language vs Dwight's matter-of-fact response. The fax detail is perfectly bureaucratic.

jokes.text: One topping, no tippin'.
jokes.setup: Hermes offering to take everyone out for pizza
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals his cheapskate conditions
jokes.explanation: Classic Hermes cheapness. Great economy - four words undercut his generosity perfectly.

jokes.text: It's great. Very flat.
jokes.setup: Restaurant host welcoming customers to their 'nice table'
jokes.punchline: Underwhelming description of table as 'very flat'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan. The mundane quality being promoted as a selling point is absurd.

jokes.text: It's-a so tender, you don't even gotta chew! Oh, good! My battery was running low.
jokes.setup: Praising how tender the pepperoni is
jokes.punchline: Bender treats it as a charging opportunity rather than food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - takes food description literally for robot purposes. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Let's see... pepperoni, or pepperoni? Would you like to hear today's special? It's pepperoni.
jokes.punchline: Every option is pepperoni, including the special
jokes.explanation: Standard 'limited menu' gag. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Sweet coconut of the shogunate! Why aren't you at the Young Bureaucrats Club?!
jokes.setup: Hermes discovering Dwight working instead of at club
jokes.punchline: Bizarre exclamation mixing tropical and feudal Japanese elements
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Hermes exclamation. The randomness of 'coconut of the shogunate' is inspired.

jokes.text: Well, you found the worst possible way. By tellin' me!
jokes.setup: Dwight apologizes for not knowing how to tell him
jokes.punchline: Hermes declares that actually telling him was the worst method
jokes.explanation: Good ironic logic - direct honesty being the worst approach. Fits Hermes' personality.

jokes.text: Never let anyone tell you what to do. And go and get us some refills on them Arnold Palmers, chop chop.
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with demanding order
jokes.explanation: Classic contradiction structure. The immediate hypocrisy is well-executed timing.

jokes.text: I used to work in a pizza parlor, and I turned out.
jokes.punchline: Fry's life is clearly not a success story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry obliviousness. He clearly didn't 'turn out' well but lacks the self-awareness to realize it.

jokes.text: Stay tuned for our hard-hitting, unbiased report, after this word from... Fishy Joe's. It's fish-licking good!
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by sponsor message from the subject
jokes.explanation: Good media satire about conflicts of interest. The timing makes the hypocrisy clear.

jokes.text: ♪ Force a fish stick in your mouth ♪ ♪ When you come to Fishy Joe's ♪ ♪ Where the fish is never frozen and the 'frigerator broke ♪
jokes.punchline: Aggressively unappetizing descriptions and broken refrigerator
jokes.explanation: Good parody of terrible fast food marketing. 'Force' and broken fridge are nice touches.

jokes.text: I won't stand by as greedy mom-and-pop joints-- like that one-- serve better food at lower prices.
jokes.punchline: Admits they serve better food at lower prices while calling them 'greedy'
jokes.explanation: Excellent corporate villain logic - accidentally admits the competition is better while demonizing them.

jokes.text: I'd like to shove a fish stick where the star of this-a solar system don't-a shine.
jokes.punchline: Overwrought astronomical way of saying 'where the sun don't shine'
jokes.explanation: Creative alien twist on familiar phrase. The astronomical specificity is delightfully overwrought.

jokes.text: We struggle for years just to find-a pizza recipe that doesn't make-a human metabolism say, 'Get it outta me!'
jokes.punchline: Admits their food was previously inedible to humans
jokes.explanation: Good alien restaurant owner humor - casual admission of terrible past food. The quote from human metabolism is a nice touch.

jokes.text: How about you? You're literally any other candidate. Me? A humble alien with legislative experience on her home planet, and a can-do spirit? I can-a no do.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Leela run for office, she lists qualifications
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts 'can-do spirit' with 'I can-a no do'
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay contradiction. Leela's instant self-defeat after listing qualifications works for her character.

jokes.text: I promise to make-a good government the same way I make-a good pizza, with lots-a hard work, and whatever kinda mushroom is-a the cheapest.
jokes.setup: Making inspiring political promise about hard work
jokes.punchline: Undercuts it with admission of using cheapest ingredients
jokes.explanation: Good political speech parody. The honest admission of corner-cutting undermines the inspiring opener.

jokes.text: You and me both.
jokes.setup: After introducing 'Boxy Robot' as a candidate
jokes.punchline: Anchor breaks character to express confusion/dismay
jokes.explanation: Brief meta moment where anchor acknowledges absurdity. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: They're fattening up babies in the basement and choppin' up the chubbiest ones to make that sweet, innocent pepperoni! People are saying. How many people? A lot. One of the biggest numbers.
jokes.setup: Making wild accusations about baby murder
jokes.punchline: Vague attribution and nonsensical 'biggest numbers' claim
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about unsubstantiated claims. 'One of the biggest numbers' is perfectly absurd political doublespeak.

jokes.text: This is-a crazy! I don't even know how to hatch a baby!
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands basic human reproduction terminology
jokes.explanation: Good alien misunderstanding of human biology. Leela treating babies like eggs is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: No one could believe such insane lies. You'd have to be a gullible, deranged moron! Yee-haw!
jokes.setup: Fry confidently dismissing the crowd's gullibility
jokes.punchline: Immediate crowd enthusiasm proving his point wrong
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke. Fry's confidence immediately undermined by crowd reaction. Classic setup/payoff.

jokes.text: First of all, I need the exercise. But also, they're making babies into pepperoni!
jokes.punchline: Admits exercise is his primary motivation, baby murder secondary
jokes.explanation: Good priority inversion - treating exercise as more important than stopping alleged baby murder. Honest absurdity.

jokes.text: No basement, no bathroom. Not even a ceiling in-a some places.
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly basic infrastructure problems
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from specific (no basement) to general infrastructure failure. Shows the restaurant's true state.

jokes.text: You think you can cover up the truth with a bunch of obvious facts? Well, I got a mob who says otherwise!
jokes.punchline: Dismisses 'obvious facts' as cover-up, appeals to mob logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of conspiracy thinking. 'Obvious facts' being dismissed as cover-up is perfect anti-logic.

jokes.text: We can't even prove we don't-a got a restaurant a-license!
jokes.setup: Worrying about proving they don't have a basement
jokes.punchline: Reveals they can't prove basic legal compliance either
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing deeper problems. Shows the restaurant's complete lack of documentation.

jokes.text: File not found. I tried my best!
jokes.punchline: Immediate failure followed by defensive claim of effort
jokes.explanation: Simple competence failure joke. Amy's defensive 'tried my best' after instant failure is in character.

jokes.text: I won't let this happen! I mean, I'll let the second part happen.
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he's fine with Dwight joining bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Hermes' priorities showing through his heroic declaration. Self-aware correction.

jokes.text: Actually, son, we're going on a thrillin' adventure! Which might involve a few forms being stamped.
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts with boring bureaucratic reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes - can't help but make bureaucracy sound exciting. The honest addendum deflates the adventure promise.

jokes.text: What's the holds-up with my pizza? I'm-a sorry, we're a-short mandibled. We lost our cheap child labor human.
jokes.punchline: 'Short mandibled' instead of 'short handed' and casual admission of child labor
jokes.explanation: Good alien anatomy pun and casual admission of illegal employment practices

jokes.text: It requires zero skill! That's why I'm so good at it.
jokes.setup: Claiming pizza work requires no skill
jokes.punchline: Proud of being good at something that requires no skill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - proud of succeeding at something requiring no skill. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I've never washed them before. It's just... I couldn't bear to part with them. Not even for 45 minutes. They're one of the only things I have left from my old life before I got frozen.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he doesn't wash his pants
jokes.punchline: Reveals deep sentimental attachment to dirty clothes
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - turns gross hygiene into touching backstory. Perfect Fry emotional logic.

jokes.text: No! The stains was all there was!
jokes.punchline: Realizes stains were what made them special
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to pants setup. The realization that stains were the sentimental value is perfectly absurd and touching.

jokes.text: Oh, Hermes! My eyes must be goin'. I thought you were Zsa Zsa Gabor. Who? I don't know. Just laugh.
jokes.setup: Old man mistakes Hermes for celebrity
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know who that is, tells them to laugh anyway
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about outdated references. The 'just laugh' instruction is self-aware and funny.

jokes.text: That wasn't a joke.
jokes.punchline: Flatly states it wasn't actually funny
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan follow-up. Hermes' literal-mindedness works perfectly here.

jokes.text: Whoa... it's a total no-man's land. Sounds like my weekend.
jokes.punchline: Bender compares it to his boring weekend
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender self-deprecation. Functional but not special.

jokes.text: It's like a vast paper forest. What's a forest?
jokes.punchline: Kid from future doesn't know what forests are
jokes.explanation: Good environmental joke about the future. Dwight's ignorance of forests adds dark worldbuilding.

jokes.text: You want a granola bar or somethin'?
jokes.setup: In middle of dangerous filing expedition
jokes.punchline: Casually offers snacks like a normal outing
jokes.explanation: Good mundane moment in dramatic situation. Hermes being practical dad in adventure setting.

jokes.text: It may look that way to the uninitiated, but to a seasoned bureaucrat, this is art... borderin' on the erotic. Dad! I said bordering!
jokes.punchline: Gets uncomfortably aroused by paperwork, defensive correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment - sexual attraction to bureaucracy is perfectly in character and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: He got lost in his own files and died. And alas, the secrets of his system died with him.
jokes.setup: Explaining what happened to great bureaucrat
jokes.punchline: Died from his own organizational system
jokes.explanation: Good ironic death - killed by his own expertise. Sets up current situation nicely.

jokes.text: I should never have brought you here. It's too excitin'. It really isn't.
jokes.punchline: Dwight flatly contradicts with reality
jokes.explanation: Good father-son dynamic. Dwight's deadpan reality check vs Hermes' fantasy of bureaucratic excitement.

jokes.text: But that thing's gonna inflict at least 2d12 damage. We'll never survive without armor! How about chain mail?
jokes.setup: RPG combat discussion about needing armor
jokes.punchline: Literally making chain mail from paper clips
jokes.explanation: Good RPG/office supply crossover. The literalness of making actual chain mail from office supplies works.

jokes.text: We're not complacent. Show us the basement! Told ya it rhymed.
jokes.punchline: Proud that 'complacent' and 'basement' rhyme (they don't really)
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about bad rhyming and mob mentality

jokes.text: Wow, you're a hungry, hungry hypocrite. Hey, a guy's gotta eat! Actually, you don't.
jokes.setup: Calling out hypocrisy, protestor defends himself
jokes.punchline: Bender points out robots don't need to eat
jokes.explanation: Good character logic - Bender's robotic perspective puncturing human excuse.

jokes.text: No way! I didn't put nine hours into these paper-clip codpieces for nothin'!
jokes.punchline: Hermes refuses to waste his elaborate crafting effort
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of armor joke - the time investment and specific 'codpieces' make it funnier. Very Hermes.

jokes.text: Incorrect. In the hands of a bureaucrat, paperwork can be lethal.
jokes.punchline: Hermes treats paperwork as deadly weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes line - his bureaucratic expertise weaponized. Great character integration and setup for action.

jokes.text: Why are you shiverin'? It's not even cold in here. It's kinda hot. I know, but it just looks really cold.
jokes.punchline: Visual appearance trumps actual temperature
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke about white paper looking like snow. The psychological temperature effect is relatable.

jokes.text: Initial here and list the cause of death as 'stupid father.'
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating cause of death entry
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor - bureaucrat filing paperwork even for his own death. The self-blame adds character depth.

jokes.text: Get in quick before you die of heat stroke!
jokes.punchline: Actually rescuing from heat, contradicting appearance
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier temperature confusion joke. Confirms the visual vs actual temperature gag.

jokes.text: But the history books say you were lost in a whiteout. I still am!
jokes.setup: Questioning historical account of being lost
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he's still lost, just surviving
jokes.explanation: Good literalism - the legend was technically accurate, just incomplete. Nice twist on the rescue.

jokes.text: Here's a hint. There is no system! Can we get another hint? I'm a fraud!
jokes.setup: Asking for hint about legendary filing system
jokes.punchline: Admission of complete fakery, escalating to fraud confession
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and structure. Each 'hint' makes it worse. The fraud admission is a perfect punchline.

jokes.text: I said I'd get to it on Monday, but Monday never came... 'cause my calendar was under one of the piles!
jokes.punchline: Circular problem - can't find calendar to know when Monday is
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic joke. The self-defeating nature of the filing problem is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: So you created a filing system so unsystematic that literally nothing could be done without you? You truly are the greatest bureaucrat who ever lived.
jokes.punchline: Hermes treats this as the highest praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes worldview - inefficiency and gatekeeping as highest bureaucratic virtues. Great character insight.

jokes.text: Have you tried this door? You can't use that! It's for emergencies.
jokes.punchline: Emergency door was there the whole time but can't be used
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bureaucratic logic - emergency door exists but can't be used because it's for emergencies. Perfect absurd rules.

jokes.text: I knew that stuff I made up was true.
jokes.punchline: Claims his lies were actually true
jokes.explanation: Good villain logic - retroactively claiming credit for accidental truth. Nice economy.

jokes.text: Without my pants, life isn't worth living anyway.
jokes.punchline: References earlier pants trauma as motivation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to pants subplot. Fry's dramatic despair over laundry is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: No self-respecting devil worshiper would sacrifice babies in this dump!
jokes.punchline: Even satanists have standards about cleanliness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - even devil worshipers having cleanliness standards. Great unexpected angle.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Don't you mean, 'Oh, my Satan'?
jokes.punchline: Bender references earlier devil worship joke
jokes.explanation: Weak callback attempt. The connection feels forced rather than organic.

jokes.text: This address has been zoned as a pizzeria for a millennium. They must have just kept building them one on top of another, on top of another, until they built the second one, and they were done.
jokes.setup: Explaining multiple pizzerias at same location
jokes.punchline: Counts: 'one on top of another, on top of another' = two total
jokes.explanation: Great Fry math logic. The escalating description that adds up to just two is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Should I put them on? I think I speak for everyone when I say, yes, dammit, yes!
jokes.punchline: Hermes' emphatic response to pants question
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to pants subplot. Hermes' desperate enthusiasm works well.

jokes.text: I remember it like it was yesteryear... only, we didn't hang pepperoni from the ceiling. We just stuck it on a high shelf to keep the laziest rats away.
jokes.punchline: Only kept away lazy rats, not determined ones
jokes.explanation: Good detail about past food safety standards. The 'laziest rats' qualifier is a nice touch.

jokes.text: It's a log of dead flies!
jokes.punchline: Thousand-year-old fly paper became pepperoni
jokes.explanation: Excellent gross-out reveal. The thousand-year fly paper aging into 'tender' pepperoni is perfectly disgusting and clever.

jokes.text: At least it's not babies.
jokes.punchline: Finding the bright side in disgusting situation
jokes.explanation: Good perspective joke - fly meat being preferable to baby murder. Nice callback to main plot.

jokes.text: That's 92.5% of all I could ask for.
jokes.setup: Son says he thinks dad is cool despite boring job
jokes.punchline: Bureaucrat gives precise percentage of satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes - can't even express parental satisfaction without bureaucratic precision. Great character moment.

jokes.text: I swear before God almighty that these pants will never see the inside of a washing machine.
jokes.punchline: Vows to never wash them, learning nothing from experience
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Fry learning exactly the wrong lesson from his experience. Perfect callback.

jokes.text: No. No, no, no, no! What is it? Pineapple!
jokes.setup: Fry's horror at something on pizza
jokes.punchline: Pizza purist outrage at pineapple topping
jokes.explanation: Standard pineapple pizza controversy joke. Functional ending but predictable and overused reference.
